feedingProfile.preferredNpk: high-potassium (e.g. tomato-type) once flower buds form; slow-release all-purpose at several-true-leaves stage

commonIssues: Direct-sow only after the last spring frost once soil has warmed to at least 10C (50F); seedlings and plants are frost-sensitive.

Container plants dry out fast and may need daily watering in hot weather; check the top 5cm (2in) of soil and water when dry. In-ground plants are more drought-tolerant and need deep watering roughly weekly, especially 20 days before and after flowering.
recommendedMinLiters (11 L) targets dwarf cultivars (30-60cm). Tall and giant cultivars (2-4m+) are really in-ground plants; a container under ~38 L will stunt them and they can become top-heavy.
Giant cultivars (over ~2.4m / 8ft) typically need staking or a sturdy cane for support against wind and heavy seed heads; dwarf and semi-dwarf cultivars usually do not.
Long taproot prefers a deep bed (loosen soil ~60cm deep); transplant-shock prone, so direct sowing is preferred.

source.citation: Clemson Cooperative Extension HGIC - Let's Grow Sunflowers: 6 to 8 hours of direct sunlight preferable; sow after frost danger passes and soil warmed above 50F (10C); well-draining soil pH 6.0-7.5; slow-release fertilizer, heavy feeders; large plants may need support for wind/top-heaviness.
Clemson Cooperative Extension HGIC - Let's Grow Sunflowers: germination after soil warms above 50F (10C); pH 6.0-7.5; slow-release fertilizer for heavy feeders.

RHS Growing Guide - Helianthus (sunflowers): full sun; annuals are thirsty and want consistently moist soil; container plants dry out faster, water when compost feels dry, even daily in hot weather; feed with potassium-rich tomato fertiliser to encourage flowering; sow indoors late March, outdoors mid-April to May; transplant only after frost risk passes; giant types (over 2.4m) need sturdy cane support.
RHS Growing Guide - Helianthus (sunflowers): potassium-rich tomato fertiliser to encourage flowering for annual types; dwarf varieties (30-60cm) suit patio containers.
RHS Growing Guide - Helianthus (sunflowers): giant varieties (over 2.4m/8ft) need support; dwarf types generally do not; frost sensitivity - transplant only once soil warming and no frost risk.

University of Minnesota Extension - Sunflowers: enjoy full sun; well-drained soil pH 6.0-6.8; direct seed after all danger of frost passes; water regularly 20 days before and after flowering; once established water deeply but infrequently, about once a week with several gallons; all-purpose slow-release side dressing at several-true-leaves; giant (8ft+) varieties may need staking, semi-dwarf (3-8ft) usually do not.
University of Minnesota Extension - Sunflowers: planting in northern US/Canada falls April to mid-June; spacing 6in to 2ft by size; full sun.

Old Farmer's Almanac - Sunflowers: sow directly after last spring frost once soils warm to at least 50F (10C); northern US/Canada April to mid-June, South mid-March to early April; thrive in slightly acidic to alkaline soil pH 6.0-7.5; containers should hold several gallons of potting mix, 12-14in diameter pot is adequate; check soil 2in down and water when dry.
Old Farmer's Almanac - Sunflowers: planting windows by region after last frost (North April to mid-June; South mid-March to early April).

Confidence note: baseIntervalDays (3) is a conservative container-oriented estimate. Sources give qualitative watering guidance (RHS: water containers when compost dry, even daily in heat; UMN: in-ground roughly weekly with several gallons) rather than a single fixed interval; 3 days with a 2x hot-weather multiplier brackets both container and in-ground regimes. heatStressC (35C) and maxSunHours (14) are reasonable horticultural estimates, not directly cited figures.

Confidence note: container volumes (recommendedMinLiters 11, ideal 19-38) are converted from the Almanac's 'several gallons / 12-14in pot' guidance for dwarf cultivars; tall/giant cultivars are effectively in-ground plants and any single container minimum is an approximation (see commonIssues).
